protocol: GSP Track A pre-registration — exp8_v2 (hypercube Leg-4) and exp9_v2 (chiral Leg-4) redesigns
binding_rule: These criteria, thresholds, and operator selections are FROZEN. Any post-hoc tuning of thresholds, criteria definitions, or operator choice invalidates the pre-registration and must be documented as a new preregistration with user approval.

exp8_v2.title: Hypercube Leg-4 M_L kink test, three-null version
exp8_v2.hypothesis: Δ(ε)/√n on a signed hypercube has a kink at ε = M_L that is absent on all three geometry-killed nulls.

nulls.description: Erdős–Rényi G(2^n, p) with p = n / 2^n matching hypercube average degree
nulls.destroys: cube connectivity, coordination structure, Huang signature

nulls.description: Hypercube graph with Huang signs replaced by i.i.d. ±1 per edge
nulls.destroys: Huang sign pattern (source of λ_max = √n bound)

nulls.description: Configuration-model random graph with same degree sequence as hypercube

kink_detection.M0_description: Single-slope linear regression on Δ(ε)/√n vs ε across full eps_grid
kink_detection.M1_description: Two-slope piecewise linear with knot FIXED at ε = M_L (knot is NOT data-determined)
kink_detection.strength_metric: slope_change = |slope_right - slope_left| at the fixed knot in M1

pass_criteria_ALL_REQUIRED.description: Kink slope_change ratio (cube / null) ≥ 3 at n ≥ 11 for all three nulls independently
Cube kink slope_change is strictly monotonically increasing across n ∈ {10,11,12,13}. On each null the slope_change is consistent with zero-slope in n (Spearman |ρ| ≤ 0.3).
pass_criteria_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: Pre-registration avoids committing to an exact predicted exponent for the M_L signature; a strict monotone increase on cube versus flat on all nulls is a weaker but fully testable claim. If Track B requires an exponent, add it in a separate preregistration amendment.

pass_criteria_ALL_REQUIRED.description: Cube ΔBIC ≥ 10 AND at least one null has ΔBIC ≤ 2

verdict_rules.LEG4_PASS: C1 AND C2 AND C3 pass
verdict_rules.LEG4_FAIL: any of C1, C2, C3 fails
verdict_rules.LEG4_AMBIGUOUS_NOT_ALLOWED: no 'partial' verdict in v2 — every recipe returns PASS or FAIL

exp8_v2.consequence_if_fail: Hypercube is REMOVED from the GSP scoped-exhausted roster. GSP doc and CLAUDE.md updated to reflect removal. No public claim may cite hypercube as a scoped example until a future pre-registered test passes.

exp9_v2.title: Chiral-RMT soft-edge test — Path B: genuinely chiral operator
exp9_v2.hypothesis: σ_min of a genuinely chiral random matrix lands in chiral-RMT universality: (1) microscopic universality of σ_min · W after numerical floor-filtering, (2) soft-edge kink strength ≥ bulk kink strength at every W, (3) flavor-oscillation range is stable or growing in W.

primary.description: M × N bipartite Gaussian random matrix, M = 2W, N = W, entries ~ N(0, 1/√W). Compute σ_min via SVD.
primary.rationale: Standard chiral-RMT ensemble with known soft-edge behavior.

fallback.description: [[0, X], [X^dagger, 0]] block operator, X a W × W Gaussian matrix with entries ~ N(0, 1/√W). Compute σ_min = min positive eigenvalue.
fallback.rationale: Physical Dirac-operator form; equivalent universality class but different discretization. Used only if bipartite_gaussian fails due to implementation issue, not if physics disagrees.

operator_selection.rule: Run primary. Only fall back if the primary has an implementation blocker documented in the rerun log.

numerical_guards.rule: Realizations with σ_min < floor are EXCLUDED from criterion 1 universality check. Log exclusion count per W. If > 5% excluded at any W, flag as methodology issue and report separately before verdict.

criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.description: CDF of σ_min · W (after floor-filtering) converges across W. Test: Kolmogorov-Smirnov distance between empirical CDFs at consecutive Ws.
criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.threshold: max KS distance across consecutive W pairs ≤ 0.1
criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.rationale_for_0.1: Typical threshold for CDF-convergence in RMT universality tests at n=100 realizations.

criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.description: At each W, sigma_kink_at_soft strength / sigma_kink_at_bulk strength ≥ 1.
criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.note: This is the SAME criterion that cleanly rejected the Ulam-Galerkin doubling-map in exp9_v1. It is the real discriminator for chiral-soft-edge class.

criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.description: Flavor oscillation range should not monotonically shrink with W. Test: fit range ~ W^β via log-log regression; require β ≥ -0.1 at 2σ.
criteria_ALL_REQUIRED_FOR_CONFIRMATION.rationale: v1 criterion passed on monotonically-decaying range (0.53→0.33) which is consistent with noise averaging. v2 requires stable or growing range as true oscillation signature.

verdict_rules.CHIRAL_CLASS_CONFIRMED: C1 AND C2 AND C3 pass
verdict_rules.CHIRAL_CLASS_WEAK: C2 passes but C1 or C3 fails — operator has the discriminating property but does not fully converge in the tested W range

exp9_v2.consequence_if_reject: Neutrino slot remains empty in GSP scoped roster. If no chiral operator in the atlas produces CONFIRMED, the scoped-slot is formally DELETED from the GSP roster and the principle doc updated.
exp9_v2.consequence_if_weak: Report as 'chiral-class signature observed, universality not demonstrated at tested W range' — not sufficient to cite in any public claim about the scoped roster.

track_B_gate.rule: Track B pilot (QC ↔ #30 Sidon and QC ↔ #20 sunflower Leg-4 tests) MAY NOT BEGIN until exp8_v2 and exp9_v2 have been executed per this preregistration and results have been reviewed by the user.
track_B_gate.rationale: The Leg-4 template for the 10 QC edges must inherit the three methodological upgrades proven in Track A: (a) geometry-killed null (not a different geometric system), (b) pre-registered thresholds with no post-hoc tuning, (c) model-selection kink test (no peak-finding artifacts). Running Track B before Track A lands risks contaminating 10 artifacts with the same flaws that produced exp8 and exp9 v1 failures.

amendments.rule: Any change to thresholds, criteria, operators, or n/W ranges after this file is committed must be recorded as a new JSON in this directory named TRACK_A_PREREGISTRATION_AMENDMENT_<date>.json, citing the specific change and the reason. The original file remains authoritative.
